full_name: Standard Specification for Low and Intermediate Tensile Strength Carbon Steel Plates
scope: Covers four grades (A, B, C, D) of carbon steel plates of structural quality for general application, distinguished by tensile strength level. Grades C and D are the most commonly stocked. Steel is made by open-hearth, basic-oxygen, or electric-furnace process.

chemical_composition.note: Heat analysis, maximum percent unless a range is given.

mechanical_properties.note: For plates wider than 24 in., the elongation requirement is reduced by two percentage points.

license_note: Composition and mechanical property values are widely republished on free manufacturer/distributor datasheets (Octal Metals, SSAB). No paywalled ASTM standard text was accessed or reproduced.
